greet.text: Leon looks up from a plate of something fried and regards you with the enthusiasm of a man being handed someone else's problem. "Newmark. Two-a-Day said you'd be in." He wipes his fingers with ceremonial slowness. "You actually gonna run tonight? Word is you've never once jacked anywhere that mattered."

options.text: Ask what he knows about the target.

wilson.text: "Kid, 'wilson' isn't a phase, it's a diagnosis." A couple of Gothicks at the rail snicker on cue. Leon leans in, marginally kinder. "Free advice: hotdoggers who borrow ice they can't buy are called chalk outlines. But Two-a-Day pays my rent, so."

options.text: Ask what he knows about the target.

target.text: Leon's eyes do a slow lap of the room before he answers. "Nothing. That's the thing. Industrial node, no name, no traffic, ice rated way past what a nothing node needs." He shrugs. "Two-a-Day says it's a soft test run. Two-a-Day says a lot of things."

codes.text: He palms you a chip printed with a run vector and entry codes, greasy thumbprint included at no charge. "Coordinates, entry string, and a time window that closes at three. After that the codes rot." He goes back to his plate. "Try not to die, Newmark. Corpses are bad for the room."

bye.text: "Mm," says Leon, which is Barrytown for goodbye, good luck, and you're already forgotten, all in one syllable.
